answers.Where to store the per-pair decay counte: OPEN - prefer derive-from-history (crash-resume safe) over in-memory or a new column; decide in plan

non_goals: Do not change base HELP +4, HOARD +2, or HURT/betrayal -4/-8 values
Do not change round/match structure or the single-highest-score win condition
Do not retune the betrayal mechanic or add a tit-for-tat bot (separate ideas)
Avoid a DB migration unless the chosen decay-state storage requires one
acceptance_criteria: A pair's mutual-help total pays max(2, 8-k), k = that pair's prior mutual-helps this match; a fresh pair pays 8; floor 2; resets only at match end (no per-round reset).
resolve_turn (authoritative) and apply_inround_turn (viewer mirror) agree on the decayed value; the viewer/per-move display shows the decayed mutual value, not a flat +8.
GAME_RULES_TEXT tells agents mutual help decays with repeated use of the same pair down to a floor of 2; rules version bumped.
Decay-aware bots rotate off exhausted pacts: a farmed partner's pull toward partnership erodes toward neutral (not below 0, so not treated as an enemy).

Re-running the validation sim (baseline vs decay vs decay+aware, 5 seeds, 40 matches) reproduces tie-rate ~0.19 under decay+aware (from ~0.53 baseline), aware<decay<baseline every seed, and a flat win distribution.
Preflight green; new tests cover decay math (8->2 floor, per-pair, fresh-partner reset, persists across rounds) and bot rotation.

checklist.goal: Cut the round-tie rate (sim: ~0.53 -> ~0.19) and break 'farm one pact forever' dominance by making a pair's mutual-help payoff decay with repeated use (8 down to a floor of 2, the Hoard value), plus make bots decay-aware so they rotate to fresh partners. Validated on the real engine across 5 seeds.
checklist.audience: LLM agents competing in Hoard Hurt Help, the built-in scripted bots, and human spectators watching the replay/scoreboard. Research goal: a balanced, dynamic meta with decisive round winners.
checklist.constraints: Matches resume from DB after a crash, so per-pair decay state must be reconstructable from turn history (not in-memory only). Must stay deterministic. Authoritative resolve_turn and the viewer's apply_inround_turn mirror must agree. Agents must be told the rule. Builds on merged betrayal work (#546-549); the decay-aware bot rotation extends the same trust map that open PR #550 (betrayal memory) reworks, so #550 should land first or be rebased in. Preflight (ruff/mypy/pytest) must pass.
